etymology.description: 禾(禾) (hé, 'grain') + 工(工) (gōng, 'work') = phonetic 'zōng' -> phonetic component
etymology.interpretation: The character 㚇 combines 禾(禾) ('grain') with 工(工) ('work') to form a phonetic component 'zōng'. This implies a combination of agriculture and craftsmanship, though the emphasis here is phonetic rather than semantic.
